methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $12M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $1.5B - $4.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Ultima Genomics
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $4B

private.name: Insitro
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $2.4B

private.name: Colossal Biosciences
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.5B

data.narrative:
Cellares, a Series C-stage biotech company, is valued against a peer universe comprising three public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and eight private peers in Biotech & Health, including same-stage companies Ultima Genomics ($4.0B), Insitro ($2.4B), and Colossal Biosciences ($1.5B).

Two valuation methods are applicable. The Stage-Progression method, using $12M total raised as a proxy base and applying a 1.7x Series C step-up multiplier, implies $20M. The Peer-Median approach anchors to three same-sector, same-stage private comparables with a median valuation of $2.4B and a range of $1.5B to $4.0B. Public-Comp Anchoring cannot be applied because Cellares' revenue is not disclosed. The triangulation of these two methods produces a low estimate of $17M, a median of $1.2B, and a high of $2.8B.

The substantial gap between the Stage-Progression result ($20M) and peer-median results ($1.5B–$4.0B) reflects the coarseness of raised capital as a valuation proxy versus observable transaction multiples for comparable private companies. The peer-median range of $1.5B to $4.0B likely represents the most defensible estimate, with key uncertainty around Cellares' relative commercial progress, IP defensibility, and capital efficiency relative to Ultima Genomics, Insitro, and Colossal Biosciences.
